skill.name: testing-fundamentals
skill.description: Auto-invoke when reviewing test files or discussing testing strategy. Enforces testing pyramid, strategic coverage, and stack-appropriate frameworks.
skill.summary: Guides test reviews, coverage strategy, and framework choices for practical software testing.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell backticks, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns. Review found Markdown code fences, TypeScript test examples, and testing terminology only; no executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Classified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and testing examples, not Ruby or shell execution. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are documentation blocks or TypeScript testing examples. No command runner, subprocess call, or shell invocation appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings refer to a description line and a TypeScript describe block. No cryptographic operation is present. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line 155 token is part of a test suite example, and line 3 is skill metadata. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, cipher use, or hashing code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Heading
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a testing pyramid heading. It does not enumerate system data or inspect the host. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a testing strategy diagram title. No filesystem, environment, process, network, or host discovery behavior appears near this location.

content.value_statement: Teams often write tests that are slow, brittle, or too focused on implementation details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward practical testing strategy and review feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews test files for behavior-focused assertions and brittle implementation checks.
Recommends unit, integration, and end-to-end test balance using the testing pyramid.
Maps common stacks to suitable tools, including Vitest, Jest, React Testing Library, pytest, and Playwright.
Provides Socratic review questions for mentoring developers on test strategy.
Identifies common testing anti-patterns such as over-mocking and large snapshot tests.
limitations: Does not execute tests or measure real coverage by itself.
Does not provide full framework documentation for every testing library.
Does not replace project-specific quality gates or release criteria.
Focuses on general testing fundamentals rather than specialized compliance testing.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Request Tests
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether new tests cover user behavior, edge cases, and failure states without brittle selectors.

use_cases.title: Coach Testing Strategy
use_cases.description: Guide a junior developer toward the right mix of unit, integration, and end-to-end tests.

use_cases.title: Plan Coverage for a Feature
use_cases.description: Choose the minimum valuable tests for critical flows before a feature ships.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Test File
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this test file for behavior coverage, edge cases, and brittle assertions. Suggest clear improvements using testing fundamentals.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Testing Level
prompt_templates.prompt: For this feature, decide which tests should be unit, integration, or end-to-end. Explain the tradeoffs and recommended tools.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Weak Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this feature and its current tests. Identify missing happy paths, edge cases, and error states that matter most.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a focused testing strategy for this project. Include framework choices, review standards, anti-patterns to avoid, and release confidence criteria.

output_examples.input: A React component test checks internal state and CSS classes.
output_examples.output: The review recommends testing visible behavior instead. It suggests role-based queries, user events, and assertions on user-facing states.

output_examples.input: A new checkout feature has only happy path tests.
output_examples.output: The guidance adds payment failure, empty cart, validation, and recovery cases. It keeps full end-to-end tests limited to critical flows.

output_examples.input: A Vite project needs a testing stack recommendation.
output_examples.output: The response recommends Vitest for unit tests, React Testing Library for component integration, and Playwright for critical journeys.

best_practices: Ask what user behavior the test proves before adding assertions.
Keep most tests fast and isolated, with fewer full end-to-end flows.
Mock external boundaries, but keep application logic and interactions real where possible.
anti_patterns: Writing tests only to increase coverage numbers.
Testing private state, CSS classes, or library internals instead of behavior.
Mocking every dependency until the test no longer proves the feature works.

faq.question: Does this skill run my test suite?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and strategy. A separate tool must execute tests.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Vitest, Jest, React Testing Library, Playwright, pytest, and go test at a practical level.

faq.question: Can it help with missing tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps identify happy paths, edge cases, and error states that should be tested.

faq.question: Is it useful for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives review criteria for brittle selectors, over-mocking, snapshots, and implementation-focused tests.

faq.question: Does it enforce 100 percent coverage?
faq.answer: No. It recommends strategic coverage focused on critical paths and meaningful failure cases.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains testing choices through simple questions and concrete review criteria.
